articles.title: Antimatter's Secret Wave Trick Finally Caught on Camera!
articles.summary: Scientists just proved that positronium—a weird atom made of matter AND antimatter—can act like a wave. A team in Japan shot it through graphene and saw a wavy interference pattern for the first time ever.

articles.title: When AI Bots Try Teamwork, Chaos Erupts!
articles.summary: AI agents—smart chatbots that can do tasks on their own—are terrible at working together. A social network full of bots turned into a scammy mess, and even a hand-picked team couldn't agree on a simple logo. Scientists say bots are too agreeable and don't listen to experts.

articles.title: Musk vs. Altman: The $134 Billion Courtroom Showdown!
articles.summary: Elon Musk is suing OpenAI boss Sam Altman, saying he was tricked into funding a nonprofit that later turned for-profit. The trial starts this week in California, and Musk wants $134 billion in damages—plus to kick Altman out of the company.
